Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class MainWindow

Namespace
Pandowdy.UI
Assembly
Pandowdy.UI.dll

Main application window for the Pandowdy Apple IIe emulator.

public class MainWindow : ReactiveWindow<MainWindowViewModel>, INotifyPropertyChanged, IDataContextProvider, IThemeVariantHost, IResourceHost, IResourceNode, ISetLogicalParent, ISetInheritanceParent, ISupportInitialize, IStyleable, INamed, IDataTemplateHost, ISetterValue, IRenderRoot, ICloseable, IStyleHost, ILogicalRoot, ILogical, ITextInputMethodRoot, IInputRoot, IInputElement, IFocusScope, ILayoutRoot, IViewFor<MainWindowViewModel>, IViewFor, IActivatableView

object.ToString()

Remarks

⚠️ Construction: Do not construct this class directly. Use MainWindowFactory to create properly initialized instances with all dependencies injected.

Architecture: This class serves as the "View" in the MVVM pattern, handling UI-specific concerns like window lifecycle, keyboard focus management, and bridging between the view model and emulator core components.

Dependency Injection Constraint: Avalonia requires windows to have a parameterless constructor for XAML loading. To work around this limitation while maintaining testability and avoiding service locator anti-pattern, this class uses a two-phase initialization:

  1. Constructor: Parameterless, called by XAML loader, minimal initialization
  2. Initialize(): Called immediately after construction by MainWindowFactory, injects dependencies
Both phases are handled automatically by Create().

Key Responsibilities:

  • Window lifecycle management (OnOpened, OnClosed)
  • Emulator thread management (start/stop/reset)
  • Keyboard input routing and accelerator handling
  • Settings persistence (save/restore window size and preferences)
  • Focus management (keeping keyboard focus on display)
  • Bridging view model commands to emulator actions

Thread Coordination: Manages cross-thread communication between:

  • UI thread (Avalonia dispatcher)
  • Emulator thread (Task.Run background thread)
  • Refresh ticker (60 Hz UI updates)

Usage Example: 

// Correct: Use the factory
var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>();
var mainWindow = factory.Create();
mainWindow.Show();

// Incorrect: Manual construction
var mainWindow = new MainWindow(); // Missing dependencies!

Constructors

MainWindow()

Initializes a new instance of the MainWindow class.

public MainWindow()

Remarks

⚠️ Important: Do not construct MainWindow directly. Use MainWindowFactory instead, which properly handles two-phase initialization and dependency injection.

XAML Requirement: This parameterless constructor is required by Avalonia's XAML loader. Heavy initialization and dependency injection are deferred to the Initialize(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker) method.

Setup:

  • Loads XAML via InitializeComponent()
  • Registers menu pointer event handlers for focus management
  • Does NOT attach machine or frame provider (deferred to Initialize)

Call Sequence: This constructor is called first by the XAML loader or MainWindowFactory, immediately followed by Initialize(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker) (managed by MainWindowFactory).

Recommended Pattern: 

// Correct: Use factory
var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>();
var window = factory.Create();

// Incorrect: Manual construction (missing dependencies!)
var window = new MainWindow();

Properties

IsCapsLockEnabledForInput

Gets whether caps lock emulation is currently enabled.

public bool IsCapsLockEnabledForInput { get; }

Property Value

bool

True to convert lowercase to uppercase, false to preserve case.

Remarks

Exposed to Apple2Display control for keyboard input processing. The Apple IIe keyboard was uppercase-only, so this defaults to true for authenticity.

Methods

Initialize(MainWindowViewModel, VA2M, IFrameProvider, IRefreshTicker)

Initializes the MainWindow with all required dependencies. Must be called immediately after construction (handled by MainWindowFactory).

public void Initialize(MainWindowViewModel viewModel, VA2M machine, IFrameProvider frameProvider, IRefreshTicker refreshTicker)

Parameters

viewModel MainWindowViewModel

Main window view model containing UI state and commands.

machine VA2M

Apple IIe emulator machine instance.

frameProvider IFrameProvider

Provider supplying rendered video frames.

refreshTicker IRefreshTicker

60 Hz ticker for driving display updates.

Remarks

⚠️ Important: Do not call this method directly. Use Create() which handles the complete construction and initialization sequence correctly.

Two-Phase Initialization: This method completes the initialization started by the parameterless constructor, injecting all dependencies that couldn't be provided through constructor parameters due to Avalonia's XAML requirements.

Why Two-Phase? Avalonia requires windows to have parameterless constructors for XAML compilation. Since we can't inject dependencies via constructor, we use this separate Initialize() method called by MainWindowFactory immediately after construction.

Initialization Steps:

  1. Validate single initialization (throw if called twice)
  2. Set ViewModel and DataContext
  3. Store machine and refresh ticker references
  4. Attach machine and frame provider to Apple2Display control
  5. Initialize SoftSwitchStatusPanel with its view model
  6. Set up ReactiveUI subscriptions for view model property changes
  7. Bridge command executions to emulator actions
  8. Restore settings from configuration file

Reactive Subscriptions: Subscribes to view model properties and propagates changes to emulator and display:

  • ThrottleEnabled: Controls CPU speed (1.023 MHz vs unthrottled)
  • CapsLockEnabled: Controls keyboard uppercase conversion
  • ShowScanLines: Controls CRT scanline effect
  • ForceMonochrome: Controls color vs monochrome display
  • DecreaseContrast: Controls contrast reduction
  • MonoMixed: Controls mixed mode text defring

Command Bridging: Links view model commands to code-behind event handlers:

  • StartEmu → OnEmuStartClicked
  • StopEmu → OnEmuStopClicked
  • ResetEmu → OnEmuResetClicked
  • StepOnce → OnEmuStepOnceClicked

Recommended Pattern: 

// Let the factory handle everything:
var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IMainWindowFactory>();
var window = factory.Create(); // Calls both new MainWindow() and Initialize()

Exceptions

InvalidOperationException

Thrown if Initialize is called more than once, or if ScreenDisplay control is not found in XAML.

InitializeComponent(bool, bool)

Wires up the controls and optionally loads XAML markup and attaches dev tools (if Avalonia.Diagnostics package is referenced).

[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]
public void InitializeComponent(bool loadXaml = true, bool attachDevTools = true)

Parameters

loadXaml bool

Should the XAML be loaded into the component.

attachDevTools bool

Should the dev tools be attached.

OnClosed(EventArgs)

Called when the window is closed (after disposal).

protected override void OnClosed(EventArgs e)

Parameters

e EventArgs

Event arguments.

Remarks

Cleanup Sequence:

  1. Dispose refresh ticker subscription
  2. Stop refresh ticker
  3. Stop emulator thread (cancel token)
  4. Call base.OnClosed()

Note: Settings are saved in OnClosing, not here, because by this point the window's platform implementation has been disposed and we can't access window properties.

Clean Shutdown: Ensures all background threads and subscriptions are properly disposed to prevent resource leaks and allow graceful application exit.

OnClosing(WindowClosingEventArgs)

Called when the window is closing (before disposal).

protected override void OnClosing(WindowClosingEventArgs e)

Parameters

e WindowClosingEventArgs

Cancel event arguments (can cancel close).

Remarks

Shutdown Sequence:

  1. Save window position/size to configuration file
  2. Save display settings to configuration file

Important: Settings are saved in OnClosing (not OnClosed) because by the time OnClosed fires, the window's platform implementation has been disposed and we can't access Position, Width, Height, or Screens Properties.

Settings Persistence: Saves both window geometry (position/size) and display settings (scanlines, colors, etc.) to separate configuration files for cleaner organization and independent loading. Uses tracked "normal" bounds if window is maximized to ensure proper restoration.

OnGotFocus(GotFocusEventArgs)

Called when the window receives keyboard focus.

protected override void OnGotFocus(GotFocusEventArgs e)

Parameters

e GotFocusEventArgs

Focus event arguments.

Remarks

If the pointer is not over the menu bar, redirects focus to the Apple2Display control to ensure keyboard input is captured for emulator input.

OnKeyDown(KeyEventArgs)

Handles key down events for Apple IIe pushbutton emulation.

protected override void OnKeyDown(KeyEventArgs e)

Parameters

e KeyEventArgs

Key event arguments.

Remarks

Currently only handles Shift key for pushbutton 2. Does NOT mark event as handled so shift combinations (Shift+F5, etc.) still work.

F1 and F2 pushbutton presses are handled in HandleAccelerator() instead.

OnKeyUp(KeyEventArgs)

Handles key up events for Apple IIe pushbutton emulation.

protected override void OnKeyUp(KeyEventArgs e)

Parameters

e KeyEventArgs

Key event arguments.

Remarks

Pushbutton Mapping:

  • F1: Pushbutton 0 (game controller button)
  • F2: Pushbutton 1 (game controller button)
  • Shift (Left/Right): Pushbutton 2 (open-apple/closed-apple)

Releases the corresponding pushbutton when the key is released. Marks event as handled to prevent further processing.

OnOpened(EventArgs)

Called when the window is opened and visible.

protected override void OnOpened(EventArgs e)

Parameters

e EventArgs

Event arguments.

Remarks

Startup Sequence:

  1. Apply Windows 11 position fallback (reapply position after 100ms delay)
  2. Set keyboard focus to Apple2Display control
  3. Start the 60 Hz refresh ticker
  4. Subscribe to refresh stream and trigger display updates
  5. Post command to start emulator (begins emulation automatically)

Windows 11 Workaround: Even though we set position before Show() in MainWindowFactory, Windows 11 sometimes overrides it. This fallback reapplies the position 100ms after the window opens, which usually succeeds where the initial attempt failed.

Refresh Ticker: Subscribes to 60 Hz timing signal and calls screenDisplay.RequestRefresh() on each tick. All updates are marshaled to the UI thread via RxApp.MainThreadScheduler.

Auto-Start: Uses Dispatcher.UIThread.Post to start the emulator after the window is fully opened, ensuring smooth startup without blocking the UI thread.

OnPointerPressed(PointerPressedEventArgs)

Called when the window receives a pointer (mouse) press event.

protected override void OnPointerPressed(PointerPressedEventArgs e)

Parameters

e PointerPressedEventArgs

Pointer pressed event arguments.

Remarks

If the pointer is not over the menu bar, redirects focus to the Apple2Display control to ensure keyboard input is captured for emulator input after mouse clicks.
